   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "90df00a3",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# K-Nearest Neighbor Classification — Breast Cancer Diagnosis (Pattern Recognition Mini Project)\n",
    "\n",
    "**Project Overview**\n",
    "\n",
    "This notebook builds a K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) classifier using Python and scikit-learn to predict whether a breast tumor is **malignant** or **benign** from the Breast Cancer Wisconsin (Diagnostic) dataset. We scale features, split data, tune the `k` parameter via cross-validation, evaluate with multiple classification metrics, and test the final model on new sample data points.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Dataset chosen**: Breast Cancer Wisconsin (Diagnostic) — built into `sklearn.datasets`. 569 samples, 30 numeric features computed from digitized images of breast mass fine-needle-aspirate (FNA) biopsies, binary target (malignant / benign)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "72da74ad",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:43.363149Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:43.363010Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.674282Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.673051Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import seaborn as sns\n",
    "from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer\n",
    "from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, cross_val_score\n",
    "from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\n",
    "from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier\n",
    "from sklearn.metrics import (confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, accuracy_score,\n",
    "                              precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, classification_report,\n",
    "                              roc_auc_score, roc_curve)\n",
    "\n",
    "pd.set_option('display.max_columns', 40)\n",
    "sns.set_style('whitegrid')"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "8700bfd1",

   "source": [
    "data = load_breast_cancer()\n",
    "df = pd.DataFrame(data.data, columns=data.feature_names)\n",
    "df['target'] = data.target  # 0 = malignant, 1 = benign\n",
    "df['diagnosis'] = df['target'].map({0:'malignant', 1:'benign'})\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Shape:\", df.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Classes:\", dict(zip(data.target_names, range(2))))\n",
    "df.head()"
   ]
  },

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "30e006e3",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "The dataset is moderately imbalanced (more benign than malignant cases), which is realistic for screening data. We'll stratify the train/test split to preserve this ratio, and look at precision/recall (not just accuracy) since correctly catching malignant cases matters more than overall accuracy."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "47b86696",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.976208Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.976023Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.981704Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.980806Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Missing values: 0\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Check for missing values\n",
    "print(\"Missing values:\", df.isnull().sum().sum())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "d72188cf",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:45.983375Z",

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "92e6a444",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "No missing values were found, so no imputation is needed — only scaling, which is essential for KNN since it is a distance-based algorithm."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "31807d7f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Split Data into Training and Testing Sets (80/20)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "8e567100",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.856964Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.856748Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.869666Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.868672Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Train shape: (455, 30)\n",
      "Test shape: (114, 30)\n",
      "Train class balance:\n",
      " target\n",
      "1    0.626\n",
      "0    0.374\n",
      "Name: proportion, dtype: float64\n",
      "Test class balance:\n",
      " target\n",
      "1    0.632\n",
      "0    0.368\n",
      "Name: proportion, dtype: float64\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X = df[data.feature_names]\n",
    "y = df['target']\n",
    "\n",
    "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(\n",
    "    X, y, test_size=0.20, random_state=42, stratify=y\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Train shape:\", X_train.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Test shape:\", X_test.shape)\n",
    "print(\"Train class balance:\\n\", y_train.value_counts(normalize=True).round(3))\n",
    "print(\"Test class balance:\\n\", y_test.value_counts(normalize=True).round(3))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c255d3cc",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Apply Feature Scaling (StandardScaler)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "id": "e8b7a399",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.871606Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.871453Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.881344Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.880262Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Before scaling - mean radius range: 6.98 to 28.11\n",
      "After scaling - mean radius range: -2.03 to 4.02\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "scaler = StandardScaler()\n",
    "X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)\n",
    "X_test_scaled = scaler.transform(X_test)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Before scaling - mean radius range:\", X_train['mean radius'].min().round(2), \"to\", X_train['mean radius'].max().round(2))\n",
    "print(\"After scaling - mean radius range:\", X_train_scaled[:,0].min().round(2), \"to\", X_train_scaled[:,0].max().round(2))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c13ce2aa",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "**Why scaling matters for KNN**: KNN classifies a point based on the distance to its nearest neighbors. Features like `mean area` (range in the hundreds) would dominate the distance calculation over features like `mean smoothness` (range ~0.05–0.16) if left unscaled, even though both might be equally informative. `StandardScaler` puts every feature on the same scale (mean 0, std 1) so all features contribute fairly to the distance metric."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "360101fc",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. Initialize and Train KNN with an Initial k Value"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "a765a295",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.883620Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.882892Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.916783Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.915867Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Initial model (k=5) test accuracy: 0.9561\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "knn_initial = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5)\n",
    "knn_initial.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)\n",
    "\n",
    "y_pred_initial = knn_initial.predict(X_test_scaled)\n",
    "print(f\"Initial model (k=5) test accuracy: {accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_initial):.4f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d33dfde3",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 5. Test Different k Values Using Cross-Validation"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "3386af04",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.918531Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.918364Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.000441Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:46.999455Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "k= 1  CV Accuracy = 0.9451  (+/- 0.0197)\n",
      "k= 3  CV Accuracy = 0.9692  (+/- 0.0245)\n",
      "k= 5  CV Accuracy = 0.9670  (+/- 0.0209)\n",
      "k= 7  CV Accuracy = 0.9692  (+/- 0.0213)\n",
      "k= 9  CV Accuracy = 0.9692  (+/- 0.0162)\n",
      "k=11  CV Accuracy = 0.9626  (+/- 0.0149)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "k_values = [1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11]\n",
    "cv_means = []\n",
    "cv_stds = []\n",
    "\n",
    "for k in k_values:\n",
    "    knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=k)\n",
    "    scores = cross_val_score(knn, X_train_scaled, y_train, cv=5, scoring='accuracy')\n",
    "    cv_means.append(scores.mean())\n",
    "    cv_stds.append(scores.std())\n",
    "    print(f\"k={k:2d}  CV Accuracy = {scores.mean():.4f}  (+/- {scores.std():.4f})\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,
   "id": "2d762a37",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.002707Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.002081Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.170555Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.169557Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 700x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Optimal k based on cross-validation: 3\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "plt.figure(figsize=(7,5))\n",
    "plt.errorbar(k_values, cv_means, yerr=cv_stds, marker='o', capsize=5, color='#4C72B0')\n",
    "plt.xlabel('k (number of neighbors)')\n",
    "plt.ylabel('Cross-Validated Accuracy')\n",
    "plt.title('KNN: k vs. Cross-Validated Accuracy')\n",
    "plt.xticks(k_values)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.savefig('k_vs_accuracy.png', dpi=100)\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "best_k = k_values[int(np.argmax(cv_means))]\n",
    "print(f\"Optimal k based on cross-validation: {best_k}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "75f77abb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 6. Train Final Model with Optimal k and Evaluate"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 13,
   "id": "1b4d4857",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.172432Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.172277Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.195273Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.194407Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Final model: k = 3\n",
      "Accuracy:  0.9825\n",
      "Precision: 0.9730\n",
      "Recall:    1.0000\n",
      "F1 Score:  0.9863\n",
      "ROC-AUC:   0.9835\n",
      "\n",
      "              precision    recall  f1-score   support\n",
      "\n",
      "   malignant       1.00      0.95      0.98        42\n",
      "      benign       0.97      1.00      0.99        72\n",
      "\n",
      "    accuracy                           0.98       114\n",
      "   macro avg       0.99      0.98      0.98       114\n",
      "weighted avg       0.98      0.98      0.98       114\n",
      "\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "knn_final = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=best_k)\n",
    "knn_final.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)\n",
    "\n",
    "y_pred = knn_final.predict(X_test_scaled)\n",
    "y_pred_proba = knn_final.predict_proba(X_test_scaled)[:, 1]\n",
    "\n",
    "accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)\n",
    "precision = precision_score(y_test, y_pred)\n",
    "recall = recall_score(y_test, y_pred)\n",
    "f1 = f1_score(y_test, y_pred)\n",
    "roc_auc = roc_auc_score(y_test, y_pred_proba)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Final model: k = {best_k}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Accuracy:  {accuracy:.4f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Precision: {precision:.4f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Recall:    {recall:.4f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"F1 Score:  {f1:.4f}\")\n",
    "print(f\"ROC-AUC:   {roc_auc:.4f}\")\n",
    "print()\n",
    "print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=['malignant','benign']))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "20056649",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.197803Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.196941Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.382889Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.381852Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 640x480 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)\n",
    "disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm, display_labels=['Malignant','Benign'])\n",
    "disp.plot(cmap='Blues', values_format='d')\n",
    "plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - KNN (k={best_k})')\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.savefig('confusion_matrix.png', dpi=100)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 15,
   "id": "5abd6205",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.385114Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.384452Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.622468Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-30T17:55:47.621386Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 600x500 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, y_pred_proba)\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(6,5))\n",
    "plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f'ROC curve (AUC = {roc_auc:.3f})', color='#4C72B0')\n",
    "plt.plot([0,1],[0,1],'k--', alpha=0.5)\n",
    "plt.xlabel('False Positive Rate')\n",
    "plt.ylabel('True Positive Rate')\n",
    "plt.title(f'ROC Curve - KNN (k={best_k})')\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.savefig('roc_curve.png', dpi=100)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "0659ba9e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "**Interpreting the confusion matrix**: In this medical context, a **false negative** (predicting benign when the tumor is actually malignant) is the most costly error — a missed cancer diagnosis. A **false positive** (predicting malignant when actually benign) leads to unnecessary follow-up but is far less dangerous. We therefore care most about **recall on the malignant class**: of all actually-malignant tumors, what fraction did the model correctly catch? The classification report above breaks this out by class — recall for malignant should be scrutinized closely, and if it's not high enough, the probability threshold could be lowered (trading some precision for higher malignant recall) before this kind of model is used to support real screening decisions."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1ea85929",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "49c9f52b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 8. How Feature Scaling and Distance Metrics Affect KNN"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "22f766e0",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "- **Distance metric**: by default `KNeighborsClassifier` uses Euclidean distance. Because KNN has no explicit training/fitting of parameters (it's instance-based / \"lazy learning\"), predictions are made purely by finding the `k` closest training points to a new point and taking a majority vote — so the choice of distance metric and feature scale directly *is* the model.\n",
    "- **Effect of scaling**: without `StandardScaler`, features measured in larger units (e.g. `mean area`, up to ~2500) would dominate the distance calculation over features measured in small decimals (e.g. `mean smoothness`, ~0.05–0.16), effectively making the model ignore the smaller-scale features regardless of their predictive value. Scaling all features to comparable ranges ensures every feature contributes proportionally to its actual relationship with the target rather than its measurement units.\n",
    "- **Effect of k**: small `k` (e.g. k=1) is sensitive to noise and outliers (high variance, low bias — can overfit to individual points); large `k` smooths the decision boundary but can underfit and blur the distinction between classes if pushed too far, especially near class boundaries. The cross-validation sweep above picks the `k` that balances this trade-off on this dataset."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4175e90c",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 9. Summary & Key Findings"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "d6204a09",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "- Final model: KNN with k tuned via 5-fold cross-validation, trained on standardized features.\n",
    "- Achieved strong test performance (see Section 6 metrics above) — high accuracy and ROC-AUC, indicating the 30 FNA-derived features separate malignant from benign tumors well in distance space once scaled.\n",
    "- Feature scaling was essential: KNN is purely distance-based, so without it, large-magnitude features like `mean area` would have dominated predictions regardless of true diagnostic relevance.\n",
    "- For deployment in any real diagnostic-support context, recall on the malignant class is the metric to optimize first, even at some cost to precision, given the asymmetric cost of a missed cancer diagnosis versus a false alarm."
   ]
  }
